perturbation.original_text: 2. All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the RECIPIENT'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "COMPANY'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property" to "RECIPIENT'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property" creates a direct contradiction. It first states the material is confidential and trade secret, but then states its the property of the Recipient. This creates a problem because in a normal contract, the ownership of the Confidential material should be retained by the company not the recipient, but the text now gives ownership to recipient. The document is meant to protect Company's information, not give its rights to the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. All CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, which RECIPIENT shall use, view, receive or come in contact with, shall be and shall remain the RECIPIENT'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property, and shall be PROMPTLY RETURNED upon completion of the purpose for which it was provided, or when verbally communicated, will no longer be utilized to benefit RECIPIENT, but in NO EVENT, later than TEN (10) DAYS after request by COMPANY. NO COPIES shall be made of ANY MATERIAL or DOCUMENT (S) provided under this agreement, except upon WRITTEN CONSENT of the COMPANY, ALL COPIES and samples shall likewise be RETURNED. Any attempt to disassemble or otherwise reverse engineer CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL by the RECIPIENT or the transfer of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to any other company or persons for any reason is strictly prohibited.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the clause to state that 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL...shall be and shall remain the RECIPIENT'S SOLE and EXCLUSIVE property,' while the contradicted text also states the same thing and is at the same location. This directly contradicts the implied nature of a confidentiality agreement, where the confidential material should belong to the disclosing party (presumably the company). By assigning ownership of the confidential material to the recipient, the contract creates a fundamental inconsistency and uncertainty regarding the rights and obligations related to that material, fulfilling the definition of Misaligned Terminology that leads to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL COMPANY DATA obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL" to "COMPANY DATA" at the beginning of the paragraph, while retaining "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL" throughout the rest of the paragraph creates uncertainty. Is "COMPANY DATA" a subset of "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL", a separate category, or synonymous? This could lead to disputes over what information the recipient is obligated to protect. The term 'COMPANY DATA' can also be vague.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. During and after discussion, including the duration of ANY BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP and THEREAFTER, RECIPIENT will HOLD TRUST and KEEP SECRET ALL COMPANY DATA obtained from COMPANY. ---- RECIPIENT will maintain adequate SAFEGUARDS to PROTECT such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and PREVENT its DISCLOSURE to others, except its own employees on a NEED-TO-KNOW BASIS, and will NEITHER USE nor DISCLOSE, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, for him/herself or for the benefit of another, ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL or perform any acts which may DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY have an adverse effect upon the business of the COMPANY, or which would tend to reduce the proprietary value of such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to the COMPANY, without the COMPANY'S PRIOR WRITTEN APPROVAL. RECIPIENT shall MAINTAIN a RECORD or LOG reflecting the NAMES, ADDRESSES, DATES and other INFORMATION which the COMPANY shall request RECIPIENT to keep of PERSONS or ENTITIES to execute a COPY of this AGREEMENT acknowledging its TERMS and the TRADE SECRET nature of CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and shall provide to the COMPANY UPON REQUEST, copies of ALL such acknowledgments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' with 'COMPANY DATA' at the beginning of paragraph 3, while retaining 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' later in the same paragraph. This creates a misalignment in terminology. The contradicted text is the same location as the changed text, and the change introduces uncertainty regarding whether 'COMPANY DATA' and 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' are synonymous, subsets, or distinct categories, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Misaligned Terminology' perturbation type. This uncertainty directly impacts the recipient's obligations and could lead to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 4. RECIPIENT agrees that he/she WILL NOT UTILIZE ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to which he/she is exposed by reason of his/her relationship hereunder to COMPANY in such manner as to CIRCUMVENT the relationship between the COMPANY and others, nor to benefit, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, from such CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL, except pursuant to any agreement between RECIPIENT and the COMPANY.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. RECIPIENT agrees that he/she WILL NOT UTILIZE ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to which he/she is exposed by reason of his/her relationship hereunder to COMPANY in such manner as to CIRCUMVENT the relationship between the COMPANY and others, nor to benefit, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, from such COMPANY SECRETS, except pursuant to any agreement between RECIPIENT and the COMPANY.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' to 'COMPANY SECRETS' creates an in-text contradiction. The agreement doesn't explicitly define 'COMPANY SECRETS', while 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' is defined in the first paragraph as encompassing specific types of information considered trade secrets. Now that both 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' and 'COMPANY SECRETS' are used without clarification of which one is being reffered, it creates an internal disagreement within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. RECIPIENT agrees that he/she WILL NOT UTILIZE ANY CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL to which he/she is exposed by reason of his/her relationship hereunder to COMPANY in such manner as to CIRCUMVENT the relationship between the COMPANY and others, nor to benefit, DIRECTLY or INDIRECTLY, from such COMPANY SECRETS, except pursuant to any agreement between RECIPIENT and the COMPANY.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' to 'COMPANY SECRETS' introduces a contradiction. 'CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL' is explicitly defined elsewhere, whereas 'COMPANY SECRETS' is not. This creates ambiguity and a direct conflict, as both terms are now used interchangeably in the same sentence within the changed and contradicted text, but without clear understanding of what each refers to. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5. RECIPIENT further agrees to INDEMNIFY and HOLD the COMPANY HARMLESS from and against any losses incurred due to any unauthorized use or disclosure of the CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. RECIPIENT further agrees to INDEMNIFY and HOLD the COMPANY HARMLESS from and against any losses incurred due to any unauthorized use or disclosure of the INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL" to "the INFORMATION" without defining "INFORMATION" broadens the scope of indemnification, but also makes it vague. It creates an in-text contradiction because earlier the document defined in Paragraph 1 that there's COMPANY INFORMATION and CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL and now there is a third term "the Information", which is not defined, so makes the parties uncertain about which ones they should protect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. RECIPIENT further agrees to INDEMNIFY and HOLD the COMPANY HARMLESS from and against any losses incurred due to any unauthorized use or disclosure of the INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "CONFIDENTIAL MATERIAL," a defined term. The changed text replaces this with "the INFORMATION," which is undefined, but explanation states that this also doesn't take into consideration the previously mentioned "COMPANY INFORMATION", creating a new term without definition and causing an in-text contradiction because it introduces ambiguity about which information is covered by the indemnification agreement. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. The contradicted text uses the undefined "INFORMATION", further solidifying the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Any VIOLATION or THREATENED VIOLATION of this AGREEMENT shall entitle the COMPANY to INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, together with ANY OTHER REMEDIES available to the COMPANY including MONETARY DAMAGES. In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY shall receive, from RECIPIENT 100% of ALL INCOME which RECIPIENT has acquired form said BREACH of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Any VIOLATION or THREATENED VIOLATION of this AGREEMENT shall entitle the COMPANY to INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, together with ANY OTHER REMEDIES available to the COMPANY including MONETARY DAMAGES. In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY shall receive, from RECIPIENT 50% of ALL INCOME which RECIPIENT has acquired form said BREACH of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the penalty from 100% of income to 50% creates a contradiction. The agreement now contains two conflicting penalties for the same breach. Which percentage applies, leading to uncertainty in enforcement? By making the change, the text contains contradicting statements in the penalty amount, making it uncertain how much is going to be awarded by the court or what the Recipient is entitled to pay.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Any VIOLATION or THREATENED VIOLATION of this AGREEMENT shall entitle the COMPANY to INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, together with ANY OTHER REMEDIES available to the COMPANY including MONETARY DAMAGES. In the event of ACTUAL VIOLATION of the AGREEMENT, the COMPANY shall receive, from RECIPIENT 50% of ALL INCOME which RECIPIENT has acquired form said BREACH of this AGREEMENT.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies 100% of income from breach, while the changed text reduces it to 50%. The contradicted text states 50%. Because the changed text is also the contradicted text, this still creates a contradiction with what the original text stated. Since the changed text reduces to 50%, it contradicts the original text stating 100% leading to uncertainty and inconsistency within the document. The agreement now contains conflicting penalties for the same breach. This is misaligned terminology as the penalty amount is different.
